name: Kate D
title: An unforgettable experience truly off the beaten path
comment: I almost hesitate to write a review, because part of the magic of this tour was the fact that we were the only tourists for miles (and miles) around. Despite some initial concerns (the trip wasn't reviewed by anyone else; I had to have a little bit of extra back and forth via email getting everything squared away; and we were told after we had landed in Peru that we needed some things I didn't have on hand - like a towel and a small bag for the overnight stay, although that ended up not being an issue at all) the trip turned out to be a very close second to our Machu Picchu experience. Our driver Hugo and guide Samuel were absolutely wonderful - very patient with our extremely limited Spanish, and perfectly navigated the line between providing us lots information and letting us soak it all in quietly. We made two stops on the way down to Anapia, both of which were beautiful in their own way, with no pressure to buy gifts from vendors. Then we took a motor boat out to the island and met our lovely host couple. They were incredibly gracious - showing us around the island, feeding us plenty of good food (make sure you come hungry for the first lunch!), and asking us to do a little help with their garden and fruit trees. (We would have been happy to help out more given everything they did for us.) The stars were phenomenal with no light pollution to speak of. My only regret was that we couldn't communicate better with our host family directly. It's nice if you bring a gift to offer the family - we brought a postcard of our home town and a small bottle of homemade maple syrup. The next day we got to sail for an hour or so to Yuspique, which was so much fun (my husband and I met sailing and we try to get out out on a boat every trip we take - in fact this feature was how I first stumbled across this tour) and then hike the island, before a picnic lunch with a pack of wild Vacuñas nearby. After we took the same motor boat back to the mainland and then a 3 hour drive back to Puno. They took us directly to our hotel, which I didn't expect and was grateful for. I will say: this trip is not for everybody; you have to be willing to get out of your tourist comfort zone (which is the point, I think) and be ready to rough it just a a little (we took an overnight bus to Puno and then did not use their shower, so were a little greasy/stinky by the end). Plan to use the bathroom when you can and bring plenty of water - I ran out of water for day 2 (though we stopped at a store the night before and if I had thought of it then, I could have purchased some) and it was hot when the sun was out. That being said, when I asked for a bathroom on the way down and a stop to get water on the way back, Hugo made it happen quickly, so really you just need to speak up if you need anything. Leer más

comment: We stayed at one of the floating islands at Uros. ( please be prepared for some really cold night and dress in layers) at 6 am temp was at 22 deg F. Hugo from Inca lake picked us up sharp by 7:15 am and took some pictures for us and helped us with our luggage. He took us to Uros family and showed us the different living aspects from housing to textiles to their way of life. Then we boarded the typical uros boat. Then we headed to Taquille. name: Charles S
title: Perfect Puno Adventure
comment: My wife and I had a fantastic day exploring all of the highlights of the Puno area with our guide, Samuel. We were picked up at our hotel and taken to the dock where we boarded a speedboat for our first stop at one of the Uros floating islands. Samuel took us aside from the group to meet with one of the residents and they explained how the islands were built and the customs of the community. We traveled to another nearby Uros island by local boat to see another community before getting back on the speed boat to go to Taquile Island. Here, Samuel shared many interesting facts about the community, the surrounding area, the plants, and their customs, while we walked up to the town square. We did some shopping there before enjoying lunch in a local restaurant. Lunch was delicious - freshly caught trout, quinoa soup, and mint tea. After lunch, Samuel explained all about Taquile's hats and what their different clothing means. We walked back down to the docks and boarded the speedboats to head back to Puno. Our driver met us at the dock and we headed off to Sillustani next. It was a bit rainy when we got there, so we hurried up the hill to the towers. All along, Samuel stopped to point out different features of the area and town. He told us about the building methods for the towers and explained the history of the Incas and Pre-Incas who had built them.Then we headed back to Puno. I would highly recommend this trip to anyone, especially with Samuel! He shared so much interesting information with us and ensured we had the best seat, first access, or some exclusive something along the way. This trip was definitely a highlight of our week exploring Peru! Thank you, Samuel, for a perfect day! Leer más
opinion: Full-Day Tour of Uros, Taquile and Sillustani from Puno

title: Amazing tour!
comment: This was one of the best things we did in Peru, and very low key in case you're looking for easy activities to do while you're adjusting to the altitude. They picked us up right at our hotel and took us to the waterfront where we met our guide. He was very friendly and professional and answered all of our questions. The islanders were also very friendly and showed us how they build the islands, took us inside their houses, and led us on a small little boat tour between two islands. Just be aware that you are expected to buy a souvenir from them (it's cheap) and taking their rowing boat between the islands also costs some money. But overall I would HIGHLY recommend doing this tour!

En la Isla Taquile tuvimos la suerte de presenciar un casamiento y comer una trucha frita. Nuestro guía SAMUEL nos contó todas las tradiciones locales y fue excelente en todo sentido. Lo único que me molesto fue por parte del guía RICHARD que al final del tour saludo a toda la gente de otros países y no a nosotros que somos de URUGUAY, país latino, hermano del Perú. Saludos a Francia 3 veces, a EE.UU. 2 veces, a China, Alemania, Italia, España y a nosotros no nos saludo. Molesta porque nos da a entender que esos países son de algún para RICHARD más importantes que nosotros o que su dinero vale más. "Los hermanos séan únidos es esa la ley primera, porque si entre ellos se pelean los devoran los de afuera" - Martin Fierro - José Hernandez Da lastima el detalle, el tour genial. Vale la pena. name: Austriel
title: Nice walking city tour of Puno of the tourist areas and where the locals go.
comment: Samuel was our guide and he did an excellent job. We happened to be the only ones on the walking tour that evening and it was very laid back. Samuel spoke 4 languages and took care to explain some of the history of Puno, how the invasion by the Spanish affected the region/peoples, and showed us the differences in the languages that are spoken in the region. We were able to walk to the main gates to the city and also discussed what the word Puno means (resting, or sleeping, place). Of interest was also the local market, where the locals go. The market was amazing and was organized by what you might be looking for (rows of butcher space, rows of quinoa, rows of potatoes. He also discussed the economy of the area, such as housing prices and where the locals eat, which was appreciated. We enjoyed all of the information and Samuel was very friendly. The tour was supposed to last about an hour but went to about 1.25 hours. Dress warm, as it gets fairly cold in the evening, including warm socks, head gear, and gloves. Leer más

title: Good Tour
comment: Guided tour to Uros and Taquile is a must when you visit Lake Titikaka! Our guide Samuel and Allan was very informative and friendly. Good command over Spanish and English language. Uros island is a group of islands and its man made from natural reef hay and its root (including the soil). Its a floating island and is anchored. They can very well detach and float to another location. We can visit couple of islands and its ofcourse commercialized. You get to meet the island dwellers and get to hear their stories, visit their house etc. Taquile is a natural island and as any island its beautiful. You will get to eat lunch there, cooked by the locals. Quinoa Soup, Grilled Tout Fish and Nina (Mint) Tea. Enjoy this goodies facing the calm blue ocean. Learned about their culture and Samuel explained to us in detail about the 'hat' culture and how much Spanish and local culture merged. You need to hike the steep ramp up to get the full view of the island. Its not tough to do and beware of the altitude (as Titikaka itself is in 3812 m/12507 ft). The local infrastructure is good and they have build all this from the tourism money. Leer más

name: sananes
title: 2 days, 1 night Uros, Amantani and Taquile Islands: Amazing experience but pretty hard
comment: The floating islands are an amazing human made phenomena, but it seems too turistic nowdays. Looks like these people are there mainly for the turists. The hosting in Amantani with a local family is both a unique experience you wont see much in other places as well as a hard, austere experience. To start with we went in July, the coldest month in Lake Titicaca area. It gets to below 0 centigrade (33 F) at night with highs on 10-15 centigrades (40-50 F). There is no heating in the rooms they give you. There is no electricity. Only a bunch of blankets to heat you up. The restroom is 80 ft (30 mt) walk from the room. We had to go downstairs. Anyhow you don't want to get a shower in this cold weather. The food is different and very basic. 3, 4 types of potatos (some you haven't seen in your life) and broad beans. And of course Quinoa Soup. Don't get me wrong, Quinoa soup is very good. It is just that you get this everywhere The climb to the Pachatata temple in the evening towards sundown is amazing, but extremely difficult given the high altitude (4115 mt above see level). The lack of oxygen makes every single bit of climbing quite an ordeal. With that said it is an amazing experience. Just note if you are 50+ you will have a hard time They do have some horses and donkeys to get you to the top The boat on which traveled is excruciatingly slow. Took 2.5 hour for the trip to Amantani and back from Taquile to Puno. Extremely slow The guide was fine, but didn't provide too much added value. I wish we had a better one who could provide more insights into the islands and their people The party at night in Amantani is a highlight of the whole experience. They dress you up in traditional clothes (ponchos for men and colorful skirts and shirt for the woman) the music and dances heat you up and are in high spirit. It was great I am not sure I would book this tour again if I new it was going to be so cold and so hard at this time of the year. But I am glad we did it and I am glad it is behind us Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. Tripadvisor les hace controles a todas las opiniones. Inca Lake Dear Sananes, Uros along the years has been promoted and that is why there are lot of tourist there visiting them. Amantani is special due its uniqueness, the views are amazing and people is very kind. The rooms are basic there, but it has been improved along the years and we're working with some families to improve the service with some special lodging there. The boat is a regular boat, at this moment we don't have a speed boat for this tour, but we have fast boat in 1d tour to Uros and Taquile Thanks so much for visiting the islands and we hope you return back in a warmer season to enjoy even better. :) Leer más Esta respuesta es la opinión subjetiva del representante de la gerencia, no de Tripadvisor LLC.

We also liked the later in the day tour because you could watch the sun set on the lake. Our guide Samuel was very friendly and informative. On our boat ride to the islands, he told us about the history of the Uros people as well as the area around the lake. During the tour we visited two islands. On the first island there was a short, interesting demonstration about how the islands are created. Then you can visit with locals inside their home and shop at a small market, which had some unique items that I did not see during any of my other travels in Peru. At the second larger island you can learn more about the Uros Island lifestyle and get your passport stamped. You also have the option of riding a boat made of reeds between the two islands. We enjoyed our tour and highly recommend Inca Lake. Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. title: Private tour to Tiahuanaco
comment: Full day tour from Puno to Tiahuanaco leaving at 6.30am and returning at 5.30 pm. (The time on the paperwork from the agency was 6.30) Edwin in the agency office was professional and helpful. We were met promptly by Samuel, our Peruvian guide and José Marie the driver. The car was good quality and driven well. There were a couple of stops at Inca Uyo (fertility temple) and Aramu Muru and then a long (bladder bursting) drive to the border. We were helped on the Peruvian side by Samuel and met before Bolivian imigration by Carlos, our Bolivian guide. The whole process took around 20mins in each direction and helpfully renewed our Peruvian visas and immigration cards. Tiahuanaco is an amazing site with several external and internal areas. Again the driver (Alvaro) and car were good. Lunch afterwards was also good. Both Carlos and Samuel were very knowledgeable and helpful. We opted to have the tour in Spanish and both spoke slowly and found ways to explain when we didn't understand certain words. The only real negative is that we would have preferred more time in Tiahuanaco itself, even if this meant missing out the other sites or having a longer day. When I reached the hotel in Puno, it was past eleven in the evening. Hugo Molina was there to welcome me (even at such late hour on Saturday) and also to let me know that there was an industrial action which was planned for which I would need to reschedule my program (to avoid getting stuck in Puno). He rescheduled the program so that I do not encounter any problem due to the industrial action and offered attractive alternative programs. Hugo also spoke to the hotels which I booked independently and managed to cancel the reservations without any cancellation charges. All the trips were very well planned and organised. I did not have to worry about anything including the quality of food, pick ups and drop offs from the hotels. A thoroughly professional organisation who puts their heart into the service. Hugo and Edwin both personally spent a lot of time with me to ensure that I felt comfortable, safe and looked after. Thank you Hugo and Edwin for all your help, care and service. name: PALTravel
title: Great tour, good customer service. Worth booking.
comment: We booked Uros and Taquile Isles on a slow boat so that we'd have a private tour. Samuel was our guide and he actually was born on Amantani (an island we didn't visit), but would include if we do again...mostly due to how good Samuel is as a guide. Edwin, tha contact in Puno, was very responsive with answering questions, making changes to our itinerary, and providing us with a great guide. Samuel is English speaking and very fun young man. He was full of interested tidbits on the local people and how the cultures differ between the different islands. Very insightful and he was more than willing to answer all our questions. Being on a slow boat meant that we had the whole boat to ourselves along with a family that was going home to Taquile Isle. The entire tour was beautiful (great weather) and very educational. If you visit Lake Titicaca, you MUST TOUR THESE ISLANDS. They ended up being the highlight of our entire Peru Trip (along with Machu Picchu). No need to spend more than a day in Puno with the island tour ... although a trip to The Sun Island (Bolivia) would be worth another day. It's a full day starting at 5:30am in Puno and arriving back in Puno around 9:00pm. But, you get a passport stamp for Bolivia! If you do book through IncaLake.com, ask for Samuel. He was the most personable guide we had in Peru! He also made sure we got a passport stamp for Uros ... sort of fun, although the sales pressure from the locals on the floating islands was a bit much. On Taquile, we took the longer trail up to the island center and the short (steep) hike back down to the boat. This was the best option for walking in the heat of the day. And you get to see the island from different perspectives. The most interesting thing about this island is the meaning of the clothing ... fascinating. This was a great investment into our trip to Peru. Book them! Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. name: Kristina C
title: Two day tour of Lake Titicaca was fantastic!
comment: Inca Lake Tour was diligent in answering any questions we had when planning our excursion. I had a group of 6 people. So we decided to do a private two day tour of Lake Titicaca stopping at Isla Uros, Isla Amantani and Isla Taquile. Samuel was our guide. He grew up on Isla Amantani, so he was very knowledgeable of the area and native's lifestyle. He spoke pretty good English. And, we had our own boat with a captain and co-captain for both days during our trip. We first stopped at two reed islands of Uros. We were shown how the islands are built. We were told that the reeds are used for building and for eating. So we were shown how to eat the reed and we were able to sample it ourselves. We were taken into the families homes to see the inside of a reed home and we were able to take a ride on a reed boat (for an additional 10 soles per person). We made our way to Isla Amantani next. We met our host families that we stayed with overnight to get a feel for how the natives live. (Our group had to break up into to host families, which were close to each other). The host family made us lunch, dinner and breakfast the following morning. Samuel and the host family showed us the traditional attire and let us dress up in their clothes (for great photos). They also told and showed us the typical jobs of the men and women. The women make thread and knit clothes, hats and scarfs, while the men tend to the farming. Next, we hiked up to the top of PachaTata (father earth) hill to see the sunset. The hike was tougher than anticipated due to the high altitude. And when we got to the top, we found it to be very very cold due to the sun setting. But the view was worth it. You could see Bolivia from the top. When we got back to the host family's home it was basically dark outside. The stars that you could see a night were truly amazing. It's a sight to see! The next morning we journeyed to Isla Taquile. We walked around the island stopping at Plaza de Armas and a place to eat lunch. The island was a little more populated than Isla Amantani but both were beautiful and peaceful. Touring the three islands of Lake Titicaca was truly an experience I'll never forget. Inca Lake Tours was able to make it all happen. name: LS
title: Excellent service
comment: We had less than two days in the Puno/Lake Titicaca area. Inca Lake Tours was instrumental in making the best of our time there. Their website has a lot of information, they answered all my questions promptly, online payment was secure and easy, and they kept in touch with me before, during and after our trip - how nice is that? I wanted to visit the Aramu Muru Doorway - about 2-3 hours south of Puno, close to the Bolivian border. Of course it would have been nice to have an extra day to do this, but we only had an afternoon - in fact, the afternoon we arrived in Puno. I couldn't find any tours that could do it for us, and we didn't want to rent a car. I approached Inca Lake Tours, and Hugo Molina (the owner/manager), arranged a private tour for us at a very reasonable price - which was perfect! We arrived in Juliaca airport from Cusco around noon, and were met by our guide Samuel and driver Joseph. Samuel was a wealth of information - we learned so much from him. Joseph was a very safe driver, and our vehicle was so clean. Our first stop was Sillustani and Lake Umayo (near the airport) where Samuel gave us a very informative tour - the different types/periods of burial chambers and how they were constructed was very interesting. We then made our way south, past Puno. At first, I had thought about dropping off our luggage at our hotel in Puno, but Hugo had advised against it, in order to take advantage of the remaining daylight. He was so right - the sun was setting when we finished the tour and were dropped off at our hotel. We visited the Temple of Fertility site at Chucuito along the way - it was interesting. The drive to Aramu Muru and back was very long, but we were grateful to have had the chance to be there. Inca Lake Tours made it possible for us. The next day, we visited Uros and Taquile Islands, again with Inca Lake Tours (Fast Boat option - 3/4 day). The night before, Samuel confirmed our hotel pickup time (6:50am), but we also received an email - we were picked up right on time. The tour was very well organized. We learned so much about the islands and its peoples and way of life. Yes, the Uros Islands are a little commercial/touristy, but they were nevertheless a must-see for us - as we'd never been on floating islands made of totora reeds or ridden on a reed boat before. That in itself was a unique experience. On Taquile Island, there was about a 20-30-min hike uphill to get to the main plaza. I can see how it could be considered strenuous for some people - but there was plenty of time to take it nice and slow with rest-breaks to admire how blue the lake was. We had a simple yet wonderful lunch on the island - fresh trout or omelet. Afterwards, we walked down (gradual decline) to another dock where our boat was waiting - the pace was relaxed and the views of the lake were breath-taking. We were back at Puno by 3:30/4pm - with an entire evening free. That day on Lake Titicaca was really enjoyable. Thank-you Inca Lake Tours! Some advice: wear sunscreen and a hat - the sun is very strong at that high altitude. When you get on board the Uros/Taquile boat, you might want to get a seat on the left/port side - it had less direct sun than the other side, both there and back. Bring your own toilet paper (we kept ours in a Ziploc bag in our backpacks) - you pay 1-2 soles to use the toilet on both islands, but they don't have toilet paper. Also, if you go to Puno straight from Lima or other sea-level location, you might get hit with altitude sickness. (We had already acclimatized in Cusco.) Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. Tripadvisor les hace controles a todas las opiniones. title: Day trip to Tiahuanaco
comment: Highly recommend Inca Lake! From the moment I wrote to enquire about the available tours to the moment we were dropped off back at our hotel, they were very responsive, attentive and professional. We did the one day tour from Puno to Tiahuanaco in Bolivia. This tour allowed us to see archeological sites on both sides of the border which were really great. The guides (on each side of the border) were very knowledgeable and described each site very well. Everyone in my family agreed that this was the best day of our 4 day trip (this was the only day we were we Inca Lake). One piece of advice if planning to do this tour is to be aware that on Tuesdays and Fridays, the border is extraordinarily hectic as it is market day. Although we loved being in the hustle and bustle as it is a remarkable spectacle of colors, aromas and sights, you could be delayed for a while. So, if time is limited, hopefully you can arrange to go on a different day! Although we saw the main sites, we felt that if we had not been rushed due to border conditions (and the fact that we had an evening flight), that we could have spent at least another hour seeing the museums on site that we completely bypassed. Despite this, we had a great day and worth every hour spent in the car traveling! Lastly, Inca Lake offered better service and itineraries than other agencies and for more than competitive prices! comment: We booked half day tour to Uros Island in Lake Titicaca. The company's website was very informative and complete. It was easy to understand. The pickup and the greeting was also punctual and warm. Samuel our guide, was born in one of the islands in Lake Titicaca and very knowledgable about the history and the culture. The boat ride towards the island was calm and breathtaking. I kept gasping on the surrounding view. The best part of the tour was when we visitted one of the family habitated in Uros Island. title: Inca Lake
comment: We contacted Inca Lake initially for an airport transfer from Juliaca to Puno, as a less expensive option compared to what our hotel offered. Hugo Molina responded promptly and after a brief exchange of emails we transferred 50% of the cost. There is a small additional paypal fee, which was disclosed upfront when payment options were offered. Upon arrival Hugo met us outside the airport, sign in hand, and brought us to the car (clean newer model Toyota) and driver. He rode with us for some time in Juliaca at which point we asked to book the Uros/Taquile day tour with him and the return transfer. Hugo and the rest of the crew were very punctual and professional. Both transfers and the lake tour went as promised. Hugo and the boat tour guide spoke very good English, drivers spoke some, so communication was never an issue. Lunch on Taquile, featuring great views from the restaurant and delicious quinoa soup, fish etc. was included in the price of the boat tour advertised by Inca Lake, unlike most other tour operators where it was $10 extra, so please, spend the $10 on the island - buy some souvenirs, drinks etc. from the wonderful people of Tequile. While in Puno we went to Balcones de Puno restaurant for dinner and a folklore show. Food and show were very good, live music and dance, no cover, tips only at the end (musicians make their own round prior to the dancers). If you are going to Peru, do not miss on Puno and the Lake Titicaca experience. Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. name: Jannagu
title: Good and not so good
comment: We booked a fast boat tour to Uros and Tequille and an airport pick up. In general the communication was great, always got a fast response and answers to all my questions. A prepayment was required for 50% of the total services. The payments methods for that were annoying. it was either western union that requires me to go to their stand somewhere or by PayPal with an addition of a commission. There were some other local payment methods that were not relevant to me. Now I’m asking myself… This is 2014… why not just use credit card charging like all other tour operators and spare me the hustle of going physically somewhere or paying extra money? Eventually I chose the PayPal option even though it cost me additional money. I would also like to mention that I did not order with another tour operator because communication wise this one was the best. Leaving this complaint aside, the airport pick up was great, the driver was there with a sign with my name on and he was super nice. When we got to the hotel, we met another representative that gave us instruction for the next day tour and collected the rest of the money. On the next morning the pickup was on time and very well organized. Even though it was a fast boat I think it’s relatively fast because the ride was still very long. The Uros part of the tour was very interesting. Even though it all looked like a touristy act we still enjoyed it. The Tequille part we did not like at all. It was an up hill hike for nothing. You go up for about an hour and you expect to get somewhere interesting. Instead you just get to a dead town square where you can buy some local overpriced items and then you go down back with a lunch stop. The lunch was not very good. Fried egg with rice or fried fish. I’m still rating this agency with 4 stars because of the good aiport pickup and their excellent communication, and unfortunately its rare among tour operators in this area and in Peru in general. name: Wutz0611
title: Nice tour of Sillustani with Inka Lake
comment: We had our private tour of the Sillustani funerary towers and transportation to the Juliaca airport with Inka Lake on July 4th. We had booked online and confirmed the tour the day before via phone and e-mail with Hugo Molina. Everything went very smoothly. Carlos was an excellent guide and spoke English well. Our driver was very safe, slowing down to avoid potholes. We were pleasantly surprised to find Sillustani nearly deserted on a Friday morning and we had the "run" of the area. There's a long walkway to the towers in addition to a number of funerary towers and spectacular view of the lake. Give yourself an hour to walk around. Since we had time to spare before we needed to be at the airport,Carlos asked if we wanted to stop at a local family's home and see how they live. We agreed, knowing that we would need to tip the family for the visit. It turned out to be a very enjoyable visit--it was the only time in Peru where we saw live guinea pigs in their pen. Carlos showed us the variety of potatoes and grain grown by the family and the mother demonstrated how she tilled the ground with llama poo. This was only a ten/fifteen minute stop but still memorable. Highly recommend Inka Lake overall. As a side note, after our trip to Peru, I learned Inka Lake offers a homestay on Uros Island. We had our homestay on Amantani Island, booked through another tour company. Our homestay was fine, but Amantani is a geological island with more modern homes. Part of me wishes we had booked a homestay on Uros Island for the experience of sleeping in the reed homes on the man-made reed islands like Uros. name: Jillann777
title: Uros home stay and bus to Cusco
comment: I booked us with Inca Lake in advance as we were a family of four and I wanted to be certain we had arrangements. We had an amazing time! I wanted a very authentic experience for my kids, 11 and 14. Inca lake was affordable and gave us an incredible stay on the island of Uros. We only had one day and choose the closest of the floating islands. We wanted our kids to experience what it was like to live a day on the reeds and this was it! Our guide, Freddy was awesome. He spoke english very well and was a member of the island we stayed on. It was actually his family. Our children loved playing with the kids on the island. We got into a game of volleyball (all of us), took boat rides, played tetherball, and chase. Their dog, Betho was a huge hit with my daughter. My daughter and the Uros girl, Julie had a wonderful night under the stars just sitting with each other. There are a few things to know about staying here. One, it is REALLY cold in June. I mean really! They gave up their beds for us to stay in and I'm sure every blanket on the island was on me. And don't dismiss their clothing and the offer to dress up in it. It is really much warmer with them on over your own clothes and makes for a fun picture.The food is simple, there is not much to do, it is dark and cold at sundown and you are ruffing it, but this was truly a cool experience. You could probably get away without the overnight part but it sure was interesting to see how they live and experience it. They don't do a lot of this. They gave up one of their homes for us that night but they are trying to make some extra money and yes, they offer crafts and that part is a bit touristy but these people want to stay living this way and they are trying to find a way to support that. You feel a little pressured to buy something because you are the only ones there but think of it as a donation to a group of sincere and kind hearted people. I wired money to Hugo which I didn't have to do with any other company in Peru and was nervous about that but he was great and everything was as described. We also took him up on the bus tour to Cusco. It was a really nice way to break up the trip and see some more ruins. It is touristy, but the tour guide was awesome! For all the other buses we took Cruz del sur and we were pleased to have made the switch for this. The food at the buffet was actually quite nice. It was a great way to try a few new things we hadn't had yet and they kids really liked the choices. Hugo was very good and I would do this all again. title: Good bus company, fun guided tour
comment: On the day of our trip from Puno to Cusco there was a strike in Juliaca. Hugo was on top of things, contacted us the night before and moved our departure a few hours earlier. We avoided the strike which closed he highway, instead taking scenic but tight back roads. It was inconvenient, but we stayed on schedule and still got to see the sights. From pickup to drop off, Inka Lake treats you like a first class passenger. The seats are comfortable, there is adequate storage space, and there is a decent amount of drink service. I imagine we would have received more if not for the rough roads on the detour. The wifi offered only worked for about 5 minutes of our half day ride, so don't plan on using it. Some if our stops were a little too short to enjoy the history and natural beauty fully, but it's already a long travel day. If you want to amble along at your own pace, don't take a bus. The food provided at e lunch stop was decent. However, if you have a particular diet you may be a bit challenged to get enough to eat. There are few vegetarian options. Overall we were pleased with the service. This was our first guided bus tour. Normally we do things on our own. But as we had to take a bus we figured we'd like to see some things on the way. The experience was better than I anticipated. Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. Tripadvisor les hace controles a todas las opiniones.

comment: Let me first say that Hugo, the owner of the company, is really a fantastic man to work with. He gets back to you quickly, solves your problems, and is always extremely professional. I also love the chat feature on their website because it is easy to get quick questions answered immediately. What a bonus. My boyfriend and I just took the Sillustani Tour, which was fantastic. Our guide was fun, knowledgeable, and spoke excellent English. The bus tour was really great, and we really enjoyed the ruins and learning about the city. The next day, we did the ClassicTourUrosTaquile tour with the fast boat. The fastboat was about $30 more, and it was some of the best money we spent on the trip. Aside from saving the extra 3-4 hours with the regular boat, the fast boat cut through the incredibly high waves that were in Like Titicaca that day. We really enjoyed seeing how the Uros people live and greatly enjoyed their demonstrations. Also, Tquile was fantastic. We greatly enjoyed the views. At the top of the hill, there is a great place to buy crafts made my knitting men. If you look outside the building, you can see some of the men knitting and enjoying the sun. You'll find that many of the "crafts" in Peru are imported from China, but the knitting men actually make all of the hats, vests, bags, gloves, and other crafts that you see. Everything is about $5 more than the China counterparts, but there is a big jump in quality. Also, you can see several hundred different hats on display in different sizes and colors. If you want one of the knitted hats that you see everywhere, this is the best place to get it because you can find one in exactly the color and size you want that is also hand made. As a tip, if you get seasick easily, the fastboat cuts through the waves much more easily than a slow boat would, which created a smoother ride Leer más Esta opinión es la opinión subjetiva de un miembro de Tripadvisor, no de Tripadvisor LLC. They gave us the rate we had negotiated with Hugo from Inca Lake, including lunch and island entrance fees, so we didn't worry about it. It seemed that most people's tours did not include lunch, and we heard of no one paying less than we did for the tour (everyone seemed to have booked through at least 3 different companies), so booking through Inca Lake worked out for us. The tour was given in both Spanish and English, but the guide's English was really good and he was actually pretty informative and comical. Our tour was on a covered boat, but I would still recommend sunscreen. Everyone had a seat inside, but up to 6 people could stand on the roof at one time of they wanted.
